acceptance.assertion: A versioned Python call-reference artifact records deterministic call sites, callee expressions, source ranges, enclosing caller symbols and explicit resolution states, bound to the canonical dump digest.
Only uniquely resolvable local definitions receive target symbol ids; attribute, alias, dynamic and ambiguous calls remain visibly unresolved or candidate-only.
MCP exposes bounded find_references and get_callers tools with exact-first matching, stable ordering, input validation, explicit truncation and live freshness on every response.
Existing find_symbol, symbol-index v1, bundle consumers and profiles remain compatible; missing call-graph artifacts fail closed without hidden refresh.
Schema, producer, consumer, MCP stdio, ambiguity, recursion, method-call, parse-error and live-bundle tests pass together with the complete Lenskit suite and GitHub CI.
A fixed navigation goldset demonstrates recovery of expected callers/references with no regression to find_symbol; default promotion remains separate.
The result makes no complete call graph, runtime reachability, dynamic dispatch, dependency completeness, test sufficiency, review or merge-readiness claim.

approval.note: Use only the isolated branch/worktree. Preserve static, read-only, freshness-bound and non-authoritative semantics.

goal: Produce a deterministic Python static call-reference index and expose bounded read-only reference/caller lookups through RepoBrief MCP with exact source ranges, freshness and explicit ambiguity.
title: Add bounded find_references and get_callers navigation
